oneOf.description: Use the localized term for "and" (e.g., "and" in English).
Use the localized symbol for "and" (e.g., "&" in English).
No conjunction (e.g., "Smith, Jones").

AndOtherOptions.description: How to render "and others" / et al.

no-page-fallback.description: Fallback policy used when page data is absent from an article-journal reference.

ArticleJournalNoPageFallback.description: Named fallback policies for page-less article-journal bibliography entries.

oneOf.description: Replace the standard article detail block with the DOI component.

BibliographyGroup.description:
A bibliography group with selector, optional heading, and per-group sorting.

Groups allow styles to divide bibliographies into labeled sections with
distinct sorting rules. Items match the first group whose selector evaluates
to true (first-match semantics).

# Examples

```yaml
groups:
  - id: vietnamese
    heading:
      localized:
        vi: "Tài liệu tiếng Việt"
        en-US: "Vietnamese Sources"
    selector:
      field:
        language: vi
    sort:
      template:
        - key: author
          sort-order: given-family
```

disambiguate.description:
Optional disambiguation scope.
- `globally` (default): Year suffixes are assigned across the whole bibliography.
- `locally`: Year suffixes are assigned independently within this group.

heading.description:
Optional heading to display above this group.
Omit for no heading (e.g., fallback group).

sort.description:
Optional per-group sorting specification.
Falls back to global bibliography sort if omitted.

template.description:
Optional per-group template override.
Falls back to global bibliography template if omitted.

BibliographyLabelMode.description: Bibliography label modes supported by scoped bibliography options.

oneOf.description: No explicit label component.
Numeric bibliography labels.
Author-date bibliography labels.

BibliographyLabelWrap.description: Wrapper punctuation supported by bibliography label options.

oneOf.description: No outer punctuation.
Parentheses.
Brackets.

compound-numeric.description: Configuration for compound numeric bibliography entries.

date-position.description: Placement of issued dates within bibliography entries.

entry-suffix.description: Suffix appended to each bibliography entry.

hanging-indent.description: Whether to use a hanging indent.

label-mode.description: Bibliography label mode for label-bearing styles.

label-wrap.description: Label wrap policy applied to bibliography labels.

multilingual.description:
Multilingual rendering defaults. Accepts a preset name (e.g., `"apa"`, `"chicago"`)
or an explicit configuration block.

punctuation-in-quote.description: Whether to place periods/commas inside quotation marks.

repeated-author-rendering.description: Repeated-author rendering mode for bibliography entries.

sort-partitioning.description: Partitioning policy for multilingual bibliography sorting and sections.

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from terms, labels, and abbreviated dates.

subsequent-author-substitute.description: String to substitute for repeating authors.

subsequent-author-substitute-rule.description: Rule for when to apply the substitute.

suppress-period-after-url.description: Whether to suppress the trailing period after URLs/DOIs.

title-terminator.description: Terminator applied to primary-title bibliography components.

volume-pages-delimiter.description: Delimiter between volume/issue and pages for serial sources.

anyOf.description: Locale general term key resolved at render time.

localized.description: Map keyed by BCP 47 locale identifiers or language tags.

BibliographyPartitionHeading.description: Localizable heading source for automatic bibliography partition sections.

BibliographyPartitionKind.description: Partition key source for bibliography partitioning.

oneOf.description: Partition by Unicode script detected from author/editor/title sort text.
Partition by the reference's effective item language.
Sort a flat bibliography by partition before normal sort keys.
Render visible sections for grouped bibliography output only.
Sort flat output by partition and render visible sections for grouped output.

BibliographySortPartitioning.description: Bibliography partitioning policy for multilingual sort order and sections.

by.description: Source used to derive each reference's partition key.

headings.description: Optional headings for visible partition sections.

mode.description: Whether partitioning affects flat sorting, visible sections, or both.

order.description: Preferred partition order. Unlisted partitions sort after listed ones.

groups.description:
Optional bibliography grouping specification.

When present, divides the bibliography into labeled sections with
optional per-group sorting. Items match the first group whose selector
evaluates to true (first-match semantics). Omit for flat bibliography.

See `BibliographyGroup` for examples.

groups-enabled.description:
Whether to apply manual `groups:` bibliography grouping.

Defaults to `true`. Set to `false` to disable the `groups:` configuration
and render a flat bibliography instead. Automatic sort-partition sections
are unaffected by this toggle.

locales.description: Locale-specific template overrides checked before the default template.

options.description: Bibliography-specific option overrides merged over the style config.

sort.description:
Optional global bibliography sorting specification.

When present, used for sorting the flat bibliography or as default
for groups that don't specify their own sort.

template.description:
The default template for bibliography entries.
Default template for entries when no localized override is selected.

template-ref.description:
Reference to an embedded template preset or external template.

If both `template-ref` and `template` are present, `template` takes precedence.

type-variants.description:
Type-specific template overrides. When present, replaces the default
template for entries of the specified types. Keys are reference type
names (e.g., "chapter", "article-journal").

CitationCollapse.description: Citation collapse behavior for multi-item citations.

oneOf.description: Collapse adjacent citation numbers into a numeric range such as `1–3`.

CitationField.description:
Discipline/field classification for a citation style.

Values correspond to the CSL 1.0 `<category field="..."/>` attribute,
`generic-base` is silently ignored during migration.

oneOf.description: Anthropology styles.
Biology styles.
Botany styles.
Chemistry styles.
Communications studies styles.
Engineering styles.
Geography styles.
Geology styles.
History styles.
Humanities styles.
Law styles.
Linguistics styles.
Literature styles.
Mathematics styles.
Medicine styles.
Philosophy styles.
Physics styles.
Political science styles.
Psychology styles.
General science styles.
Social science styles.
Sociology styles.
Theology styles.
Zoology styles.

label-wrap.description: Label wrap policy applied to citation labels.

multilingual.description:
Multilingual rendering defaults. Accepts a preset name (e.g., `"apa"`, `"chicago"`)
or an explicit configuration block.

notes.description: Document-level note marker placement and punctuation movement rules.

punctuation-in-quote.description: Whether to place periods/commas inside quotation marks.

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from terms, labels, and abbreviated dates.

volume-pages-delimiter.description: Delimiter between volume/issue and pages for serial sources.

collapse.description: Optional collapse behavior for adjacent multi-item citations.

delimiter.description:
Delimiter between components within a single citation item (e.g., ", " or " ").
Defaults to ", ".

ibid.description:
Configuration for ibid citations (ibid or ibid with locator).
Overrides fields from the main citation spec when position is Ibid or IbidWithLocator.
If present, takes precedence over `subsequent` for these positions.
Allows compact rendering like "ibid." or "ibid., p. 45".

integral.description:
Configuration for integral (narrative) citations (e.g., "Smith (2020)").
Overrides fields from the main citation spec when mode is Integral.

locales.description: Locale-specific template overrides checked before the default template.

multi-cite-delimiter.description:
Delimiter between multiple citation items (e.g., "; ").
Defaults to "; ".

non-integral.description:
Configuration for non-integral (parenthetical) citations (e.g., "(Smith, 2020)").
Overrides fields from the main citation spec when mode is NonIntegral.

note-start-text-case.description:
Optional text-case transform for standalone note-start citation output.

This is a style-owned rendering dimension layered on top of the
existing repeated-note state, not a new citation `Position`.

options.description: Citation-specific option overrides merged over the style config.

prefix.description: Prefix for the citation (use only when `wrap` doesn't suffice, e.g., " (" or "[Ref ").

subsequent.description:
Configuration for subsequent citations.
Overrides fields from the main citation spec when position is Subsequent.
Useful for short-form citations in note-based styles or author-date styles
that show abbreviated citations after the first mention.

suffix.description: Suffix for the citation (use only when `wrap` doesn't suffice).

template.description: Default template when no localized override is selected.

template-ref.description:
Reference to an embedded template preset or external template.

If both `template-ref` and `template` are present, `template` takes precedence.

type-variants.description:
Type-specific template overrides for citations. When present, replaces
the default citation template for references of the specified types.
Type-variant lookup happens after mode (integral/non-integral) resolution.
If both the main spec and the active mode sub-spec have a `type-variants`
entry for the same type, the mode-specific one wins.

wrap.description: Wrap the entire citation in punctuation. Preferred over prefix/suffix.

oneOf.description: Match only references cited in the document.
Match all references regardless of citation status.

CompoundNumericConfig.description:
Configuration for compound numeric bibliography entries.

Groups multiple references under a single citation number with sub-labels.
Used in chemistry journals (e.g., Angewandte Chemie).

collapse-subentries.description:
Whether adjacent grouped sub-entries collapse in citations.

When true, adjacent members from the same group may render as
`1a,b` or `1a-c` instead of `1a,1b` or `1a,1b,1c`.

sub-label.description: Sub-label style: alphabetic (a, b, c) or numeric (1, 2, 3).

subentry.description:
Whether grouped item citations render sub-entry labels (`1a`, `1b`).

When false, grouped item citations render the whole-group number (`1`).

contributors.description:
Contributor formatting defaults. Accepts a preset name (e.g., "apa")
or explicit configuration.

dates.description:
Date formatting defaults. Accepts a preset name (e.g., "long")
or explicit configuration.

locale-override.description:
Style-level locale override ID loaded from `locales/overrides/<id>.*`.

This patches the locale selected by `StyleInfo.default_locale` without
duplicating the full base locale. Runtime loading is limited to the
style-global config; nested citation or bibliography configs are ignored.

locators.description:
Locator rendering configuration. Accepts a preset name (e.g., "note")
or explicit configuration.

multilingual.description:
Multilingual rendering defaults. Accepts a preset name (e.g., `"apa"`, `"chicago"`)
or an explicit configuration block.

notes.description: Document-level note marker placement and punctuation movement rules.

punctuation-in-quote.description:
Whether to place periods/commas inside quotation marks.
true = American style ("text."), false = British style ("text".)
Defaults to false; en-US locale typically sets this to true.

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from terms, labels, and abbreviated dates.

titles.description:
Title formatting defaults. Accepts a preset name (e.g., "apa")
or explicit configuration.

volume-pages-delimiter.description:
Delimiter between volume/issue and pages for serial sources.
Processor adds trailing space when rendering.
Examples: Comma (APA ", "), Colon (Chicago ": ").

delimiter.description:
The delimiter between contributors. Defaults to `", "` if not specified.
`None` means "not configured at this level" and will not override an inherited value.

delimiter-precedes-et-al.description: When to include delimiter before "et al.".

delimiter-precedes-last.description: When to include delimiter before the last contributor.

demote-non-dropping-particle.description: Handling of non-dropping particles (e.g., "van" in "van Gogh").

display-as-sort.description: When to display a contributor's name in sort order.

initialize-with.description:
String to append after initialized given names (e.g., ". " for "J. Smith").
If None, full given names are used (e.g., "John Smith").

initialize-with-hyphen.description: Whether to include a hyphen when initializing names (e.g., "J.-P. Sartre").

name-form.description:
How to render given names. See `NameForm` for variants.
Per-scope overrides (per-mode, per-position) are expressed by setting
this field in the appropriate scope's contributor config block.

role.description:
When and how to display contributor roles. Accepts a preset name
(e.g., "short-suffix") or explicit configuration.

shorten.description: Shorten the list of contributors (et al. handling).

sort-separator.description:
Delimiter between family and given name when inverted (e.g., `", "` → "Smith, John").
Defaults to `", "` in the engine when not specified.
`None` means "not configured at this level" and will not override an inherited value.

ContributorConfigEntry.description:
Contributor config: either a preset name or explicit configuration.

Allows styles to write `contributors: apa` as shorthand, or provide
full explicit configuration with field-level overrides.

ContributorPreset.description:
Contributor formatting presets.

Each preset encodes the contributor formatting conventions for a major citation
style or style family. Use doc comments to describe the visual behavior so
style authors can choose the right preset without knowing style guide names.

oneOf.description:
First author family-first, "&" symbol, et al. after 20 authors,
initials with period-space, comma before "&".
Example: "Smith, J. D., & Jones, M. K."

First author family-first, "and" text, contextual serial comma,
full given names (no initials).
Example: "Smith, John D., and Mary K. Jones"

All authors family-first, no conjunction, compact initials (no
period/space), et al. after 6 of 7+.
Example: "Smith JD, Jones MK, Brown AB"

Given-first format, "and" text, initials with period-space,
comma before "and".
Example: "J. D. Smith, M. K. Jones, and A. B. Brown"

All authors family-first, "and" text, compact initials (period,
no space), comma before "and".
Example: "Smith, J.D., Jones, M.K., and Brown, A.B."

All authors family-first, no conjunction, compact initials (no
period/space), space sort-separator, et al. after 3 of 5+.
Example: "Smith JD, Jones MK, Brown AB"

Numeric compact author list for journal-heavy corpora:
all family-first, no conjunction, sort-only particle demotion,
space sort-separator, et al. after 6 of 7+.

Numeric medium author list variant:
same as `numeric-compact`, but et al. after 3 of 4+.

Numeric tight: all family-first, no initials, et al. after 3 of 7+.
Tighter than `numeric-compact` (use-first: 3 vs 6).
Example: "Smith J, Jones M, Brown A, et al."

Numeric large: all family-first, no initials, et al. after 10 of 11+.
For biomedical journals that show nearly all authors.
Example: "Smith J, Jones M, Brown A, [10 authors], et al."

Numeric all-authors: all family-first, no conjunction, compact initials,
no list shortening, particle demotion disabled.
Example: "Smith JD, Jones MK, Brown AB"

Numeric given-first with period-only initials (no space), no conjunction,
and comma delimiters.
Example: "J.D. Smith, M.K. Jones, A.B. Brown"

Annual Reviews style: all family-first, no initials, et al. after 5 of 7+,
particle demotion never. Distinguishable by "never" demote policy.
Example: "van der Berg J, Smith M, Jones A, Brown B, White C, et al."

Math/physics author-date: all family-first, period initial (no trailing
space), comma sort-separator, no conjunction. Used across Springer
math/physics and related author-date journals.
Example: "Smith, J., Jones, M., Brown, A."

Social science first-author inversion: first author family-first,
remaining authors given-first, period-space initials, comma
sort-separator, no conjunction. Common in sociology and civil engineering.
Example: "Smith, J. D., M. K. Jones, A. B. Brown"

Physics numeric given-first: no author inversion, period-space initial,
no conjunction, sort-only particle demotion. Used by numeric physics
journals like APS.
Example: "J. Smith, M. Jones, A. Brown"

approximation-marker.description: Marker for approximate dates (e.g., "ca. " or "~"). None suppresses display.

era-labels.description: Era label profile controlling which era suffixes are shown.

negative-unspecified-years.description: How negative EDTF years with unspecified digits are rendered.

open-range-marker.description: Marker for open-ended ranges (e.g., "–present"). None uses locale default.

show-seconds.description: Whether to include seconds in time display (default: false).

show-timezone.description: Whether to include timezone in time display (default: false).

time-format.description: Time display format. None suppresses time rendering.

uncertainty-marker.description: Marker for uncertain dates (e.g., "?" or "uncertain"). None suppresses display.

DateConfigEntry.description:
Date config: either a preset name or explicit configuration.

Allows styles to write `dates: long` as shorthand, or provide
full explicit configuration with field-level overrides.

oneOf.description: Abbreviated month + day + year in US order: "Jan 15, 2024".

DatePosition.description: Placement of issued dates inside bibliography entries.

oneOf.description: Immediately after the contributor component.
Immediately after the title component.
At the end of the entry.

DatePreset.description:
Date formatting presets.

Each preset defines how dates are displayed in citations and bibliographies,
including month format, EDTF uncertainty/approximation markers, and range
delimiters.

oneOf.description:
Long month names, EDTF markers, en-dash ranges.
Example: "January 15, 2024", "ca. 2024", "2024?"

Short month names, EDTF markers, en-dash ranges.
Example: "Jan 15, 2024"

Numeric months, EDTF markers, en-dash ranges.
Example: "1/15/2024"

ISO 8601 numeric format, no EDTF markers.
Example: "2024-01-15"

DelimiterPrecedesLast.description: When to use delimiter before last contributor.

Disambiguation.description:
Disambiguation settings.

Controls how ambiguous citations are disambiguated in the output.

add-givenname.description: Whether to add given names to disambiguate similarly-named authors.

givenname-rule.description: Which author positions receive given-name expansion.

names.description: Whether to attempt disambiguation by expanding author names.

year-suffix.description: Whether to append year suffixes (a, b, c, ...) for multiple works from the same author-year.

DisambiguationScope.description: Scope for disambiguation (e.g., year suffix assignment).

oneOf.description: Disambiguate across all items in the bibliography.
Disambiguate only within the current group.

DisplayAsSort.description: When to display names in sort order (family-first).

EraLabels.description: Era label profile for date rendering.

oneOf.description: Preserve current behavior: negative years use locale `before-era`, positive years unlabeled.
Negative years use locale `bc`, positive years use locale `ad`.
Negative years use locale `bce`, positive years use locale `ce`.

GeneralTerm.description:
A list of general terms for citation formatting.

These are the standard terms that appear in bibliographies and citations,
including prepositions (in, at, from, by), punctuation terms (and, et al),
date-related terms (accessed, no-date, circa), locator terms (page, chapter, volume),
and special phrases (ibid, forthcoming, available-at).

oneOf.description: The preposition "in" (e.g., "in Smith, 2020").
The term used for access dates (e.g., "accessed May 1").
The term used for retrieval statements (e.g., "retrieved from URL").
The preposition "at" (e.g., "at the conference").
The preposition "from" (e.g., "from the publisher").
The preposition "of" (e.g., "special issue of").
The preposition "to" (e.g., "from x to y").
The preposition "by" (e.g., "by John Smith").
The term used when no date is available (e.g., "n.d.").
The term used for anonymous authorship (e.g., "anonymous").
The term used for approximate dates (e.g., "circa").
The phrase used for availability statements (e.g., "available at URL").
The term used for immediately repeated citations (e.g., "ibid.").
The conjunction "and" (e.g., "Smith and Jones").
The abbreviation for omitted additional names (e.g., "et al.").
The phrase "and others" (generic use).
The term used for forthcoming works (e.g., "forthcoming").
The term used for online resources (e.g., "online").
The adverb "here".
The term used for deposited materials.
The phrase used to introduce reviewed works (e.g., "review of").
The phrase used for original publication references (e.g., "originally published").
The term used for patents (e.g., "patent").
The general term for volume locators (e.g., "volume", "vol.").
The general term for issue locators (e.g., "issue", "no.").
The general term for page locators (e.g., "page", "p.", "pp.").
The general term for chapter locators (e.g., "chapter", "ch.").
The general term for editions (e.g., "edition", "ed.").
The general term for section locators (e.g., "section", "§").
The label for personal communications (e.g., "personal communication").

GivennameRule.description:
Controls which author positions receive given-name expansion during disambiguation.

Maps to CSL's `givenname-disambiguation-rule` attribute on `<citation>`.
The engine collapses these to two scopes: `PrimaryName` and
`PrimaryNameWithInitials` expand only the first (primary) author; all other
values expand all positions. Initials vs full form is always driven by the
contributor config's `initialize-with` / `name-form` settings.

oneOf.description:
Disambiguate per-cite with a minimal subset of names (CSL 1.0.1 default).
Engine behaviour: expand all positions (per-cite minimal-subset deferred).
Expand given names for all name positions.
Expand given names (initials form) for all name positions.
Expand given name of the first (primary) author only.
Expand given name (initials form) of the first (primary) author only.

GrammaticalGender.description: Grammatical gender used for locale agreement and term selection.

oneOf.description: Masculine grammatical gender.
Feminine grammatical gender.
Neuter grammatical gender.
Common or shared grammatical gender.

Group.description:
Grouping configuration for bibliography.

Specifies how bibliography entries should be grouped in the output.

template.description: Sort keys used to define group boundaries (e.g., [Author, Year]).

anyOf.description: Locale general term key resolved at render time.

localized.description: Map keyed by BCP 47 locale identifiers or language tags.

GroupHeading.description: Localizable heading source for bibliography groups.

GroupSelector.description:
Selector predicate for matching references to groups.

All specified conditions must match (AND logic).
Use the `not` field for negation-based fallback groups.

field.description: Match references by field values (e.g., language, keywords).

not.description:
Negation for fallback groups.
Matches references that do NOT match the nested selector.

GroupSort.description:
Per-group sorting specification.

Sorting follows a template of sort keys, applied in order.
The first key is the primary sort, second is the tiebreaker, etc.

template.description: Ordered list of sort keys to apply.

GroupSortEntry.description: Citation sort configuration: either a preset name or explicit configuration.

GroupSortKey.description: A single sort key in a group sorting template.

key.description: The field or variable to sort by.

order.description:
For type-based ordering: explicit type sequence.

Example: `["legal-case", "statute", "treaty"]` for Bluebook hierarchy.
Items appear in this order regardless of alphabetical content.

sort-order.description:
For name-based sorting: culturally appropriate name order.

Example: `given-family` for Vietnamese, `family-given` for Western.

IntegralNameContexts.description: Which document contexts participate in integral citation name memory.

oneOf.description: Only body-text integral citations participate.
Body text and note citations both participate.

IntegralNameMemoryConfig.description:
Integral citation name-memory policy.

The presence of this block enables full-then-short name memory for narrative
(integral) citations. Absence disables it — there is no on/off field.

contexts.description: Which document contexts participate in name-memory tracking.

subsequent-form.description: The contributor form to use after the first mention in scope.

IntegralNameScope.description: The scope where integral citation name-memory resets.

oneOf.description: Keep one name-memory scope for the whole document.
Reset name memory at chapter boundaries.
Reset name memory at section boundaries.

et-al-marker.description: Suffix appended when truncated. Alpha/Ams default: "+", Din default: "".

et-al-min.description: Max authors before truncation. Alpha/Ams default: 4, Din default: 3.

et-al-names.description: Names shown when truncated (et-al). Alpha default: 3, Ams default: 4.

multi-author-chars.description: Chars per author family name when 2+ authors. Preset default: 1.

single-author-chars.description: Chars taken from single author's family name. Preset default: 3 (Alpha/Ams), 4 (Din).

LabelForm2.description: How a locator label is displayed.

oneOf.description: No label, bare value: "33"
Short form: "p. 33"
Long form: "page 33"
Symbol form if available in locale

LabelPlacement.description: Label placement relative to contributor names.

oneOf.description: biblatex alphabetic / BibTeX alpha.bst: up to 4 authors, "+" marker, 2-digit year.
DIN 1505-2: up to 3 authors, no et-al marker, 2-digit year.
American Mathematical Society: same label-generation algorithm as Alpha.

LabelRepeat.description: Whether labels appear on every segment, only the first, or none.

oneOf.description: Label on every segment
Label only on the first segment
No labels

LabelWrap.description: Wrapper punctuation supported by citation and bibliography label options.

oneOf.description: No outer punctuation.
Parentheses.
Brackets.
Superscript-style numeric labels.
Link the title component.
Link the URL component itself.
Link the DOI component itself.
Link the specific component this config is attached to.
Link the entire bibliography entry.

anchor.description: What text should be hyperlinked (title, url, etc.).

doi.description: Link value to the item's DOI.

target.description: The target for the link (url, doi, etc.).

url.description: Link value to the item's URL.

LocalizedTemplateSpec.description: Locale-scoped template override with optional fallback behavior.

default.description: Whether this override is the fallback when no locale matches.

locale.description: Language tags that should select this template override.

template.description: Template used when this localized override is selected.

default-label-form.description: Default label form for all locator kinds (default: Short).

fallback-delimiter.description: Fallback delimiter for unmatched compound locators (default: ", ").

patterns.description: Patterns for compound locators and type-specific rendering.

range-format.description: Range format for all locator kinds (default: Expanded).

strip-label-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from labels globally (e.g., "p." → "p").

LocatorConfigEntry.description: Preset-or-explicit entry — same pattern as DateConfigEntry.

label-form.description: Override the default label form for this locator kind.

range-format.description: Override the global range format for this locator kind.

LocatorPattern.description:
A pattern matching a specific combination of LocatorType values.

Patterns are tested in declaration order; first match wins.

kinds.description: The set of LocatorType values this pattern matches (order-insensitive).

label-repeat.description: Whether labels appear on every segment, only the first, or none.

order.description: Rendering order of segments when pattern matches.

type-class.description: Optional gate on reference type class.

LocatorPreset.description: Named presets for common locator configurations.

oneOf.description: Note style: bare page numbers, short labels for other kinds.
Author-date / numbered: short labels for all kinds.

LocatorType.description: Known locator label keyword or custom kebab-case identifier.

preferred-transliteration.description:
Ordered priority list of BCP 47 transliteration tags (e.g. `["ja-Latn-hepburn", "ja-Latn"]`).
Takes precedence over `preferred_script` when resolving transliterations.

MultilingualConfigEntry.description:
Entry for `options.multilingual`: either a preset name or an explicit config block.

Style YAML can use a short preset name:
```yaml
options:
  multilingual: chicago
```
or a full explicit block:
```yaml
options:
  multilingual:
    title-mode: combined
    name-mode: transliterated
    preferred-script: Latn
```

oneOf.description: Use original script.
Use transliteration.
Use translation matching style locale.

Combine transliteration and translation: `romanized [translated]`.
Equivalent to `Pattern([transliterated, {translated, brackets}])`.

Ordered sequence of views joined by spaces.

Use this when a style requires more than two views — e.g. Chicago's
`romanized original [translated]` or MLA's `original [translated]`.
Each segment specifies a view and an optional bracket wrap.
Segments whose resolved text is empty or identical to the previous
segment are silently skipped (dedup).

YAML form:
```yaml
title-mode:
  pattern:
    - view: transliterated
    - view: original
    - view: translated
      wrap: brackets
```

MultilingualPreset.description:
Multilingual rendering policy presets.

Each preset encodes the romanization and translation conventions for a major
citation style or style family when rendering references in an English-language
context.  Style YAML authors can write a bare preset name:

```yaml
options:
  multilingual: chicago
```

and Citum resolves it to the full [`MultilingualConfig`] at load time.

oneOf.description:
APA / Harvard / Vancouver / AMA / NLM / CSE family.

Titles: `romanized [translated]`.  Names: romanized.
Matches APA 7th-edition policy: no original CJK in bibliography;
translation required in brackets.

MLA 9th-edition policy.

Titles: `original [translated]`.  Names: romanized.
Original CJK title shown first; translation in brackets.

Chicago (Notes-Bibliography 18th / Author-Date 18th).

Titles: `romanized original [translated]`.  Names: romanized.
Romanized form is primary; original CJK follows as a supplement;
English translation in brackets for general audiences.

IEEE policy.

Titles: romanized only.  Names: romanized.
No original CJK or translation required by the IEEE style guide.

MultilingualSegment.description: A single view segment in a `MultilingualMode::Pattern`.

view.description: Which textual view to render for this segment.

wrap.description: Optional wrapping applied around the resolved text.

MultilingualView.description: Which textual representation of a multilingual field to use in a pattern segment.

oneOf.description: The original text as stored in the data.
The transliterated (romanized) form.
The translation matching the style locale.

NameForm.description:
How to render the given-name component of a contributor name.

Controls whether full given names, family name only, or initialized
given names are rendered. Used to express first/subsequent mention
differences (Chicago) and integral/non-integral differences.

Initialization formatting details (`initialize_with`, `initialize_with_hyphen`)
are separate fields and only take effect when `NameForm::Initials` is active.

oneOf.description: Render full given names: "John D. Smith".

Render family name only, suppressing given names: "Smith".
Used for subsequent mentions in Chicago/Turabian note styles.

Render initialized given names using `initialize_with` separator.
If `initialize_with` is None, defaults to ". " (e.g., "J. Smith").
Empty string gives compact initials: "JD Smith".
Display as "Given Family" (e.g., "John Smith").
Display as "Family, Given" (e.g., "Smith, John").
First contributor inverted ("Family, Given"); subsequent contributors given-first.

NameSortOrder.description: Name sorting order for culturally appropriate collation.

oneOf.description:
Family name first (Western convention).
Example: "Smith, John" → "S" sorts before "T"

Given name first (Vietnamese convention).
Example: "Nguyễn Văn A" → "Nguyễn" sorts before "Trần"

NegativeUnspecifiedYears.description: Rendering policy for negative EDTF years with unspecified digits.

oneOf.description: Render as explicit historical ranges (e.g., `-009u` → `100–91 BC`).
Reserved for future prose-oriented output; falls back to `range` if selected.

number.description: Desired location of the note marker relative to closing quotation marks.

order.description:
Whether the note marker appears before or after the closest movable
punctuation mark.

punctuation.description:
Desired location of movable punctuation relative to closing quotation
marks when note markers are introduced.

NoteMarkerOrder.description: Controls whether a note marker appears before or after adjacent movable punctuation.

oneOf.description: Place the note marker before the closest movable punctuation mark.
Place the note marker after the closest movable punctuation mark.

NoteNumberPlacement.description: Controls where a footnote number marker is placed relative to closing quotation marks.

oneOf.description: Place the note marker inside the closing quotation mark.
Place the note marker outside the closing quotation mark.

Place the note marker on the same side as the movable punctuation when
only one side has punctuation; otherwise default to outside.

NoteQuotePlacement.description: Controls where movable punctuation is placed relative to closing quotation marks.

oneOf.description: Keep movable punctuation inside the closing quotation mark.
Keep movable punctuation outside the closing quotation mark.

Follow org-cite-style adaptive behavior: punctuation stays inside when
it is already flush with the closing quote, otherwise it is placed
outside.

NoteStartTextCase.description: Text-case transform applied when a citation renders at note start.

oneOf.description: Uppercase the first character of the rendered citation.
Lowercase the rendered citation text.

NumberVariable.description: Known number variable keyword or custom kebab-case identifier.

OrgAbbreviationMemoryConfig.description:
Organizational name abbreviation expansion policy.

The presence of this block enables first-mention expansion of org names —
"World Health Organization (WHO)" on first mention, "WHO" thereafter.
Absence disables org abbreviation entirely.

contexts.description: Which document contexts participate in org-abbreviation tracking.

short-name-display.description: How to display an organizational short name on the first integral mention.

oneOf.description: Full expansion: 321-328 → 321–328
Minimal digits: 321-328 → 321–8
Minimal two digits: 321-328 → 321–28
Chicago Manual of Style 15th ed rules
Chicago Manual of Style 16th/17th ed rules

Processing.description:
Processing mode for citation/bibliography generation.

Determines how citations and bibliographies are sorted, grouped, and disambiguated.
Can be specified as a simple string or with complex configuration maps:
- A string: `"author-date"`, `"numeric"`, `"note"`, or `"label"`
- A label config map: `{ label: { preset: din } }`
- A custom config map: `{ sort: ..., group: ..., disambiguate: ... }`

oneOf.description:
Author-date styles (e.g., APA, Chicago).
Default bibliography ordering: author, year, title.

Numeric styles (e.g., IEEE, Nature).
Do not imply a bibliography sort; citations are numbered in order of appearance.

Note styles (e.g., Chicago Notes-Bibliography).
With a bibliography default to author, title, year ordering.

Label styles (e.g., Alpha, DIN 1505-2).
Default bibliography ordering: author, year, title.

Fully custom processing behavior.
Explicit `sort` configuration remains authoritative.

ProcessingCustom.description:
Custom processing configuration.

Allows explicit specification of sorting, grouping, and disambiguation rules
without relying on preset defaults.

oneOf.description: Always render full contributor names.
Replace repeated authors with an em dash.
Replace repeated authors with an em dash followed by a space.

placement.description: Where to place the label relative to names.

term.description: Locale term key for the role (e.g., "editor", "translator").

RoleLabelPreset.description: Named role-label presets for secondary contributor rendering.

oneOf.description: Suppress the configured role label.
Render the localized verb label before the names.
Render the localized short verb label before the names.
Render the localized short label after the names.
Render the localized long label after the names.

omit.description: Contributor roles for which to omit the role description.

preset.description: Global role-label preset applied before legacy compatibility.

RoleOptionsEntry.description:
Role options: either a preset name or explicit configuration.

Allows styles to write `role: short-suffix` as shorthand, or provide
full explicit configuration with field-level overrides.

delimiter.description: Custom delimiter between name parts for this script.

sort-separator.description: Custom delimiter between family and given name when this script is inverted.

use-native-ordering.description: Whether to use native ordering for this script (e.g., FamilyGiven for CJK).

SegmentWrap.description: Bracket wrapping applied to a pattern segment.

oneOf.description: No wrapping.
Wrap in square brackets: `[text]`.
Wrap in parentheses: `(text)`.

ShortNameDisplay.description: How to display a contributor's short name on the first integral mention.

oneOf.description: "Full Name (Short)" on first mention (default). For narrative/integral context.
`"Full Name [Short]"` on first mention. For parenthetical context (already inside parens).
"Short (Full Name)" on first mention.
"Short [Full Name]" on first mention.

delimiter-precedes-last.description: When to use delimiter before last name.

min.description: Minimum number of names to trigger shortening.

subsequent-min.description:
Minimum number of names to trigger shortening on subsequent cites.
Defaults to `min` if not set.

subsequent-use-first.description:
Number of names to show when shortened on subsequent cites.
Defaults to `use_first` if not set.

use-first.description: Number of names to show when shortened.

use-last.description: Number of names to show after the ellipsis (et-al-use-last).

SimpleVariable.description:
Simple string variables.

Use `variable:` for string passthrough fields, even when the field name is
also present in [`NumberVariable`]. For example, `variable: volume` keeps the
source value as plain text, while `number: volume` opts into numeric
formatting behavior.

Sort.description:
Sorting configuration.

Specifies how bibliography entries are ordered.

render-substitutions.description: Whether to apply the same name substitutions during sorting as during rendering.

shorten-names.description: Whether to shorten name lists for sorting the same as for display.

template.description: Sort keys in order of application.

anyOf.description: Explicit sort configuration with custom keys and order.

SortEntry.description:
Sort configuration: either a preset name or explicit configuration.

Can be a preset name like `author-date-title` or a full `Sort` struct with explicit settings.

SortKey.description:
Available sort keys.

Specifies what field to sort bibliography entries by.

oneOf.description: Sort by the work's author(s).
Sort by publication year.
Sort by the work's title.
Sort by citation order (typically used for numeric styles).
Sort by reference type.
Sort by author/contributor.
Sort by title.
Sort by issued date.
Sort by custom field.

SortPreset.description:
Sort order presets for bibliography entries.

Each preset encodes the sort key sequence for a citation style family.
Use for the `bibliography.sort` field to avoid repeating boilerplate key lists.

oneOf.description: Author → year → title. Standard for author-date styles (APA, Chicago, Harvard).
Author → title → year. Used in some footnote and note styles.
Citation number only. Used in numeric styles (Vancouver, IEEE).

SortSpec.description:
A single sort specification.

Defines one sort dimension with its key and direction.

ascending.description: Whether to sort in ascending order (default: true).

StyleBase.description:
A named, compiled-in style base that serves as an inheritance root.

A style file declares `extends: <key>` to inherit a complete base style.
Any top-level fields in the file (`options`, `citation`, `bibliography`,
etc.) are merged over the base, with local fields taking
ultimate precedence.

oneOf.description: Hidden Elsevier Harvard family root.
Hidden Elsevier with-titles family root.
Hidden Elsevier Vancouver family root.
Hidden Springer Basic author-date root.
Hidden Springer Basic brackets root.
Hidden Springer Vancouver root.
Hidden Taylor & Francis Chicago root.
Hidden Taylor & Francis CSE root.
Hidden Taylor & Francis NLM root.
Hidden Chicago shortened-notes root.
Chicago Manual of Style 18th edition — notes without bibliography.
Chicago Manual of Style 18th edition — author-date system.
Chicago Manual of Style (shortened notes and bibliography).
APA 7th edition — author-date system.
Elsevier Harvard (author-date).
Elsevier with Titles (numeric).
Elsevier Vancouver (numeric).
Springer Basic (author-date).
Springer Vancouver Brackets (numeric).
Springer Basic Brackets (numeric).
American Medical Association 11th edition (numeric).
Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (numeric).
Taylor & Francis Chicago author-date.
Taylor & Francis Council of Science Editors author-date.
Taylor & Francis National Library of Medicine.
Modern Language Association 9th edition (author-page).

citum-version.description:
Minimum Citum engine version required to load and render this style.

Accepts a [`semver::VersionReq`]-compatible string (e.g. `">=0.38.0"`,
`"^0.40"`). When the running engine does not satisfy the requirement,
the resolver returns
[`ResolutionError::VersionMismatch`]
instead of attempting to deserialize fields the engine may not
understand. Omit for styles that use only stable, long-lived features.

default-locale.description:
Default locale for the style (e.g., "en-US", "de-DE").
Used for locale-aware term resolution.

description.description: Short summary of the style's intended use or provenance.

edition.description:
Edition or version qualifier used alongside `short_name` to
disambiguate multiple editions of the same style family
(e.g. `"7th"`, `"18th edition"`). Omit when only one edition exists.

id.description: Stable identifier for the style, usually a URI or slug.

short-name.description:
Concise display name for the style family, used by UIs to label
search results and match banners (e.g. `"APA"`, `"Chicago Notes"`,
`"MLA"`). Omit for journal-specific styles whose full title is their
identity. Combine with `edition` to produce labels like `"APA 7th"`.

source.description: Provenance: set when this style was adapted from a CSL 1.0 source.

StyleLink.description: A hyperlink associated with a style (documentation, self-link, etc.).

href.description: Link target for related style metadata.

rel.description: Relationship type for the link, such as `self` or `documentation`.

StylePerson.description: A person credit (author or contributor) for a style.

email.description: Contact email for the credited person.

name.description: Display name for the credited person.

anyOf.description:
A URI reference to a remote or local style.

Supported schemes: `file://`, `https://`, `git+https://`, `cid:`.

StyleReference.description:
A reference to a base style, which can be either a named builtin base,
a URI (e.g., `file://...`, `@hub/...`, `https://...`, `git+https://...`),
or a content-addressed identifier (`cid:bafkrei...`).

StyleSource.description: Provenance block for styles adapted from a CSL 1.0 source.

adapted-by.description: Who performed the adaptation (e.g., "citum-migrate" or a person's name).

links.description: Links from the original CSL style (documentation, self, etc.).

SubLabelStyle.description: Sub-label style for compound numeric bibliography entries.

oneOf.description: Alphabetic sub-labels: a, b, c, ...
Numeric sub-labels: 1, 2, 3, ...
Substitute only if ALL authors match.
Substitute each matching name individually.
Substitute each matching name until the first mismatch.
Substitute only the first name if it matches.

SubsequentNameForm.description:
The contributor form used after the first integral mention in scope.

`Short` preserves non-dropping particles ("van Beethoven"); `FamilyOnly`
strips them ("Beethoven"). MLA-style narrative memory uses `Short`.

oneOf.description: Use the short contributor form for subsequent mentions.
Use family name only (without non-dropping particles) for subsequent mentions.

contributor-role-form.description: Form to use for contributor roles when substituting.

role-substitute.description:
Per-role fallback chains for non-author contributor substitution.

Keys and fallback entries normalize to canonical kebab-case role names.
Built-in template roles use locale-aware labels automatically. Custom
role names still participate in fallback and suppression even when they
do not have a dedicated template enum variant.

template.description: Ordered list of fields to try as substitutes.

SubstituteConfig.description: Substitution rules for missing author data.

SubstituteKey.description: Fields that can be used as author substitutes.

SubstitutePreset.description:
Substitute presets for author substitution fallback logic.

These presets define the order in which fields are tried when the primary
author is missing. Most styles follow the standard order, but some have
variations.

oneOf.description:
Standard substitution order: Editor → Title → Translator.
Used by most citation styles (APA, Chicago, etc.).

Editor-first: Editor → Translator → Title.
Prioritizes contributors over title.

Title-first: Title → Editor → Translator.
Used when anonymous works should show title prominently.
Editor only with short role labels.
Editor only with long role labels.
Editor then translator with short role labels.
Editor then translator with long role labels.
Editor then title with short role labels.
Editor then title with long role labels.
Editor then translator then title with short role labels.
Editor then translator then title with long role labels.

TemplateAddOperation.description: Addition operation that inserts a component before or after an anchor.

after.description: Anchor selector after which the component should be inserted.

before.description: Anchor selector before which the component should be inserted.

TemplateComponent.description:
A template component - the building blocks of citation/bibliography templates.

Each variant handles a specific data type with appropriate formatting options.

TemplateComponentSelector.description: Partial component selector used to locate anchors in a template.

TemplateContributor.description: A contributor component for rendering names.

and.description:
Override the conjunction between the last two names.
Use `none` for bibliography when citation uses `text` or `symbol`.

contributor.description: Which contributor role to render (author, editor, etc.).

delimiter.description: Custom delimiter between names (overrides global setting).

form.description: How to display the contributor (long names, short, with label, etc.).

gender.description: Explicit grammatical gender override for role-label agreement.

label.description: Optional role label configuration (e.g., "eds." for editors).

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

name-order.description:
Override the global name order for this specific component.
Use to show editors as "Given Family" even when global setting is "Family, Given".

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

shorten.description: Shorten the list of names (et al. configuration).

sort-separator.description: Delimiter between family and given name when inverted (overrides global setting).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateDate.description: A date component for rendering dates.

fallback.description: Fallback components if the primary date is missing.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateGroup.description:
A group component for grouping multiple components with a delimiter,
matching CSL 1.0 `<group>` semantics.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateModifyOperation.description: Rendering-only modification for the component matched by `match`.

label-form.description: Override the localized number label form when modifying number components.

match.description: Selector identifying exactly one component to modify.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateNumber.description: A number component (volume, issue, pages, etc.).

gender.description: Explicit grammatical gender override for number/ordinal agreement.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

show-with-locator.description:
When `true`, show this pages component even when a locator is present in a note-style citation.
By default, pages are suppressed in note-style citations when a locator is present.

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplatePreset.description:
Available embedded template presets.

These reference battle-tested templates for common citation styles.
See `citum_schema::embedded` for the actual template implementations.

oneOf.description: APA 7th edition (author-date)
Chicago Manual of Style (author-date)
Vancouver (numeric)
IEEE (numeric)
Harvard/Elsevier (author-date)
Numeric citation number only (citation-focused preset)

anyOf.description: A URI reference to a remote or local template.

TemplateReference.description:
A reference to a template, which can be either a named builtin preset
or a URI (e.g., `file://...`, `@hub/...`, `https://...`).

TemplateRemoveOperation.description: Removal operation for the component matched by `match`.

match.description: Selector identifying exactly one component to remove.

TemplateTerm.description: A term component for rendering locale-specific text.

gender.description: Explicit grammatical gender override for term selection.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

disambiguate-only.description:
When true, suppress this title component unless the reference needs
disambiguation (i.e. multiple works by the same author appear in the
document). Used by author-class styles (e.g. MLA) where the title
appears in citations only to resolve same-author ambiguity.

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

TemplateVariable.description: A simple variable component (DOI, ISBN, URL, etc.).

name-form.description: Override name form (e.g., initials, full, family-only).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

strip-periods.description: Strip trailing periods from rendered value.

suffix.description: Text to append to the rendered value (outside any wrap).

suppress.description:
If true, suppress this component entirely (render as empty string).
Useful for type-specific overrides like suppressing publisher for journals.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to the rendered value.

vertical-align.description: Vertical alignment to apply to rendered output.

wrap.description: Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes (text inside the wrap).

anyOf.description: Complete replacement template used by Template V1/V2 styles.
Structural diff applied to a parent template during style resolution.

TemplateVariant.description: Type-specific template override, either as a complete legacy template or a V3 diff.

TemplateVariantDiff.description: Structural diff that derives a type-specific template from a parent template.

add.description: Component additions applied in authored order.

extends.description: Optional parent type variant selector within the same section.

modify.description: Rendering-only modifications applied in authored order.

remove.description: Component removals applied in authored order.

TermForm.description:
Form for term lookup.

Specifies which form variant of a term should be used in citation output.

oneOf.description: Long form of a term (e.g., "page" vs "p.").
Short form of a term (e.g., "p." vs "page").
Verb form of a term (e.g., "edited by").
Short verb form of a term (e.g., "ed." vs "edited by").
Symbol form of a term (e.g., "§" for section).

TextCase.description:
Text-case transform applied to title-like fields.

Styles select which transform applies to which field category.
The engine provides the generic primitives; styles own the selection.

oneOf.description: English headline-style title case (capitalize principal words).
Generic sentence case (capitalize first word only).

APA-style sentence case: capitalize first word of main title
and first word after each subtitle boundary.

NLM-style sentence case: capitalize first word of main title only;
subtitles preserve only explicit/protected capitals.
Capitalize the first letter of the value.
Transform the entire value to lowercase.
Transform the entire value to uppercase.
No transformation; render the value exactly as stored.
12-hour clock with AM/PM (e.g., "11:30 PM")
24-hour clock (e.g., "23:30")

TitlePreset.description:
Title formatting presets.

Each preset defines how different types of titles (articles, books, journals)
are formatted. Presets typically differ in whether titles are quoted, italicized,
or plain.

oneOf.description:
APA style: article titles plain, book/journal titles italic.
Example: Article title. *Book Title*. *Journal Title*.

Chicago style: article titles quoted, book/journal titles italic.
Example: "Article Title." *Book Title*. *Journal Title*.

IEEE style: article titles quoted, book/journal titles italic.
Example: "Article title," *Book Title*. *Journal Title*.

Humanities style: monographs, periodicals, and serials all italic,
articles plain. Common in geography, history, and social sciences.
Example: Article title. *Book Title*. *Journal Title*. *Series Title*.

Journal-focused emphasis: periodicals and serials italic,
monographs plain.

Scientific/Vancouver style: all titles plain (no formatting).
Example: Article title. Book title. Journal title.

text-case.description: Text-case transform to apply to this title category.

oneOf.description: Period.
Comma.
No terminator.
The primary title of the cited work.
Title of a book/monograph containing the cited work.
Title of a periodical/serial containing the cited work.

TitlesConfig.description: Title formatting configuration by title type.

container-monograph.description: Formatting for monograph containers (book containing chapters).

TitlesConfigEntry.description:
Title config: either a preset name or explicit configuration.

Allows styles to write `titles: apa` as shorthand, or provide
full explicit configuration with field-level overrides.

TypeClass.description: A coarse reference genre used as an optional gate on locator patterns.

oneOf.description: Legal citations (e.g., "legal-case", "statute")
Classical works with traditional numbering
Standard reference types

TypeSelector.description:
Selector for reference types in overrides.
Can be a single type string or a list of types.

VerticalAlign.description: Vertical text alignment relative to the baseline.

oneOf.description: Render at the baseline (default).
Render as superscript.
Render as subscript.

WrapConfig.description:
Wrapping punctuation and optional inner affixes applied around a rendered value.

Combines the wrap punctuation with optional text that appears inside the wrap
(between the wrap character and the rendered content).

inner-prefix.description: Text inserted after the opening wrap character but before the content.

inner-suffix.description: Text inserted after the content but before the closing wrap character.

WrapPunctuation.description: Punctuation to wrap a component in.

description:
The new Citum Style model.

This is the target schema for Citum, featuring declarative options
and simple template components instead of procedural conditionals.

extends.description:
Extends a base style, with optional local overrides.

When present, the base [`StyleReference`](style_base::StyleReference) is resolved and the local
overrides are merged before any further processing. Explicit `options`,
`citation`, and `bibliography` keys at the same document level take
precedence over the resolved base.

extends-pin.description:
Optional content-addressed integrity pin for the parent style referenced
by [`extends`](Self::extends).

When present, the resolver verifies that the SHA-256 of the fetched
parent matches this CIDv1 string before merging. Mismatches abort
resolution with [`ResolutionError::IntegrityFailure`]. Absent means
"no integrity check" — appropriate for `file://` parents under user
control or trusted local registries.

title: Style
